free_text: Angular distributions for the O-16(g,p)B-11 reaction.
Lund university racetrack microtron.
Rectangular 80 x 60 mm polystyrene foils (C-8  H-8)-N
with  areal densities  of  26.3  mg/sm**2  and 26.56
mg/sm**2 were used.
Two element dE-E telescopes.
6 SI-GE telescopes.
When using  tagging  spectrometers,  each  individual
magnetic spectrometer focal-plane scattered electron
detector    defines    a   photonuclear   experiment
uncorrelated with others.  The analysis thus can  be
divided  into  two  parts.  The  first  consists  of
obtaining the kinetic energy of the reaction product
at  a  defined  detection  angle,  and the second of
identifying the energy of the  photon  inducing  the
reaction.
Statistical errors are given.
Systematic uncertainty is estimated to be about 8 %.
Data were prepared at the  MSU  INP  CDFE  by
V.V.Varlamov.
Angular differential cross sections for the  reaction
O-16(g,p)B-11   have  been  measured  for  low-lying
regions of residual excitation energy in B-11. Cross
sections   with  low  systematic  uncertainties  are
presented for proton detection angles  ranging  from
30 to 150 degree, measured with tagged photons of 25
- 75 MeV.
Data were compiled at the Russia MSU SINP
CDFE by V.Varlamov.
Corrected by V.Varlamov: DATA-ERR, dates,
lowercase.
